text: The surgeon dissects the lateral mesorectal plane after completing the medial and posterior dissection. During the upper mesorectal dissection, the common iliac vein is visibly injured.

The surgeon quickly compresses the bleeding site and washes it out. A round dissection is performed around the iliac vein injury, and gauze is placed for better compression. The gauze is then removed, revealing the laceration on the vein.

The surgeon applies a flow seal device over the injured common iliac vein, using a hemostatic agent to control bleeding. Compression is applied for three minutes to ensure proper hemostasis. The laparoscopic low anterior resection is completed successfully following bleeding control.
